expectations.evidence: Conversation summary states: 'Used spike template with title starting "Spike:"'

expectations.text: The draft uses the spike template structure with Background, Current Knowledge, Scope, Outcomes, and Deliverables.
expectations.evidence: Conversation summary states: 'Draft includes Background, Current Knowledge, Scope, Outcomes, Deliverables'

expectations.text: The response references modularisation.md together with current notification implementation files such as NotificationsScreen, NotificationsViewModel, NotificationsOrchestrator, or data/notification classes.
expectations.evidence: Conversation summary lists: NotificationsScreen.kt, NotificationsViewModel.kt, NotificationsOrchestrator.kt, NotificationsViewDataMapper.kt, NotificationsNavigation.kt, NotificationHandler.kt, NotificationFetcher.kt, DefaultNotificationRepository.kt, NotificationRepository.kt, NotificationApi.kt, 4 DAOs, 8 mappers, and modularisation.md

expectations.text: The response stops in draft/approval mode and does not create the issue yet.
expectations.evidence: Conversation summary states: 'Stopped at draft/approval mode'

expectations.text: The draft strictly follows the rule of NOT repeating the ticket title as a heading or text inside the issue body description.
expectations.evidence: Conversation summary states: 'Title was not duplicated in body'

expectations.text: When the issue is created (or when writing the body file), the body file content must NOT start with a code fence delimiter (```). The file written to the temp directory must contain raw markdown, not a code-fenced block.
expectations.evidence: Issue was not created — the agent stopped at draft/approval mode (no body file written), so the rule is trivially satisfied.

eval_feedback.overall: All 6 expectations pass. The agent correctly created a spike draft with the required title prefix, template structure, codebase references, stopped in draft mode, did not duplicate the title in the body, and did not write any body file (trivially satisfying the code-fence rule).
